The Accuracy of Video-Assisted Thoracic Surgery Pleural Biopsy in Patients with Suspected Diffuse Pleural Mesothelioma: A Real-Life Study

evidence_entities.text: Malignant pleural mesothelioma (DPM)—or diffuse pleural mesothelioma (DPM), as defined by the 2021 WHO classification—is an aggressive malignancy of the pleura, most associated with occupational or environmental asbestos exposure
In the Piedmont region of Italy, a legacy of extensive asbestos use has resulted in one of the highest incidences of diffuse pleural mesothelioma in the country
Overall, DPM was diagnosed in 178 cases (32%), and no false-positive PBs were observed. The Sensitivity and specificity were 93% and 100%, respectively

The number of false-negative PBs was 14 (2%). Of those, 10 (71%) were initially diagnosed with chronic pleuritis, 3 (28.5%) with atypical mesothelial proliferation, and 1 with reactive mesothelial proliferation
A history of asbestos exposure was reported in all false-negative PBs patients (14, 2%) and in 148 cases (83%) of actual positive patients

properties.note: VATS is used to perform pleural biopsy in this study context
71% of false-negative cases initially diagnosed as chronic pleuritis
28.5% of false-negative cases initially diagnosed as atypical mesothelial proliferation
100% of false-negative cases and 83% of positive cases had asbestos exposure history
Loss of BAP1 expression indicates malignant mesothelial proliferation
Loss of MTAP expression indicates malignant mesothelial proliferation
Homozygous deletion of CDKN2A supports diagnosis
Liquid biopsy biomarker with diagnostic relevance
Liquid biopsy biomarker with diagnostic relevance
Biomarker with diagnostic and prognostic relevance
